key_insight: N-RSAV accelerates RSAV optimization by injecting randomized low-rank curvature information via a Nyström Hessian approximation while preserving energy dissipation.
engineering_relevance: The method is aimed at ill-conditioned large-scale optimization problems, including PINN training, where faster convergence can reduce compute cost and time.
method: Approximate the Hessian with a randomized low-rank Nyström operator.
Truncate negative eigenvalues to enforce positive semidefiniteness and keep the modified energy dissipation law.
Reuse the approximate Hessian adaptively based on the gap between original and modified energies to cut overhead.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports numerical experiments on convex quadratic problems and PINN training, showing substantially faster convergence than conventional RSAV-based methods.
watch_item: The approach depends on a reliable low-rank Hessian approximation and extra assumptions for convergence guarantees under the PL condition.
paper_title: Accelerating SAV-based optimization via randomized low-rank Hessian approximation
